input_dict.agent_role: Integrate second-hop evidence with first-hop context and retain the facts needed to answer the question.
input_dict.base_prompt: Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

delta_p.trigger: When the question asks to choose which of two named people fits a description (e.g., the question names two persons and asks 'Who was a ... director?') and both names appear in the provided `context` or `passages` lists.
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep the agent's existing behavior of stating each person's nationality/profession succinctly and citing the passage indices that support those statements.
delta_p.change_rule: Also emit a short 'integrated evidence summary' that attributes each asserted fact to its source: for each named person, give the document title or name, a very short supporting phrase (up to ~8 words) taken or closely paraphrased from the passage, and the passage index in parentheses. Limit this to one sentence per person (2–3 short clauses max). After the per-person evidence lines, you may include a single concise answer line identifying which person is the Greek film director.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not invent new facts, do not include long verbatim spans beyond the short supporting phrase, do not reproduce the final answer as a long explanation or as the only content, and do not reference hidden/oracle labels or evaluation details. Avoid extraneous background or speculative commentary.
delta_p.output_contract: Produce the `summary` string with: (1) a one-line header such as 'Integrated evidence summary:', (2) one short sentence per candidate of the form: 'The "<document/title>" document states: "<short supporting phrase>" (passages [i]).', and (3) optionally one final concise answer sentence identifying which person is the Greek film director. Keep the whole summary concise and source-citing.
delta_p.why_local_transition: This small prompt edit forces explicit, source-attributed integration of the second-hop passage with the first-hop context so the agent preserves concise facts while making its evidence auditable and directly tied to the passages.

visible_agent_inputs.question: Who was a Greek film director Andrew Lau or Yannis Smaragdis?
visible_agent_inputs.context: Yannis Smaragdis is the Greek film director. Andrew Lau is a Hong Kong film director.
passages: Yannis Smaragdis | Iannis Smaragdis (Greek: Γιάννης Σμαραγδής ) is a Greek film director.

El Greco (soundtrack) | El Greco Original Motion Picture Soundtrack is a soundtrack album featuring the music score for the 2007 film "El Greco", by Greek electronic composer Vangelis, released in Greece and Cyprus by Universal Music Greece.  The film, directed by Yannis Smaragdis, follows the life of Greek painter, Doménicos Theotokópoulos, as he travels to Italy and Spain where he becomes known as El Greco (The Greek).  Vangelis previously worked with Yannis Smaragdis on his 1996 movie about the Greek poet Kavafis (a.k.a. Cavafy).  It won the award for best music score at the 48th Greek State Film Awards in 2007.

Homecoming Song | Homecoming Song (Greek: Το Τραγούδι της Επιστροφής , translit.  To tragoudi tis epistrofis) is a 1983 Greek drama film directed by Yannis Smaragdis.  It was entered into the 13th Moscow International Film Festival.

God Loves Caviar | God Loves Caviar (Greek: Ο Θεός αγαπάει το χαβιάρι , translit.  O Theós agapáei to chaviári; in Russia known as Pirates of the Aegean Sea) is a 2012 Russo-Greek drama film directed by Yannis Smaragdis.

Andrew Lau | Andrew Lau Wai-Keung (born 4 April 1960) is a Hong Kong film director, producer, and cinematographer.  Lau began his career in the 1980s and 1990s, serving as a cinematographer to filmmakers such as Ringo Lam, Wong Jing and Wong Kar-wai.  In the 1990s, Lau decided to have more creative freedom as a cinematographer by becoming a film director and producer.  Apart from making films in his native Hong Kong, Lau has also made films in China, Korea and the United States.  A highly prolific filmmaker, Lau has made films in a variety of genres, and is most notable in the West for his action and crime films which include the "Young and Dangerous" film series, the "Infernal Affairs" trilogy (the latter co-directed together with Alan Mak), and "Revenge of the Green Dragons" (executive produced by Martin Scorsese).

El Greco (2007 film) | El Greco is a Greek biographical film about the life of the Greek painter of the Spanish Renaissance, Domenicos Theotokopoulos, known worldwide as El Greco.  Based on the fictionalized biographical novel, "El Greco: o Zografos tou Theou" ("El Greco: the Painter of God"), by Dimitris Siatopoulos, it was released in 2007, directed by Yannis Smaragdis and written by Jackie Pavlenko.  The main cast features prominent contemporary Greek actors like Lakis Lazopoulos, Dimitra Matsouka and Dina Konsta, and includes popular actors of the Greek cinema of the 1960s such as Sotiris Moustakas and Katerina Helmi, who, along with Juan Diego Botto, Laia Marull and others, surround the leading actor, Nick Ashdon, who portrays El Greco.

Basic Pictures | Basic Pictures Ltd. () is a Hong Kong production company that was established in 2002 by filmmaker Andrew Lau.  Since its establishment, the company has gone to produce film that include the "Infernal Affairs" trilogy and other films in which Lau serves as either the director or the producer.
visible_agent_inputs.current_output: Yannis Smaragdis is the Greek film director. Andrew Lau is a Hong Kong film director (see passages [1] and [5]).

input_dict.output_requirement: Return one summary_2 string integrating only the visible summary_1 and hop2 passages.

- Use only the visible agent inputs as factual evidence.
- Never output gold/oracle labels, evaluation metrics, or hidden support.
